2026-04-09T01:38:46.9644278Z   1. `OperationContext.cs` - Fixed stack push order for variable-key/variable-value pairs in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy`. This was causing scope-based extension resources (like ChaosTargets, KubernetesClusterExtensions) to have reversed positional order for Id.Name and Id.ResourceType.Type parameters.
2026-04-09T01:38:46.9647044Z   2. `resource-metadata.ts` - Fixed `getExpectedParentResourceType` to handle complex parent paths with more than 3 segments. Now generates `ValidateResourceId` for nested multi-provider scopes.
2026-04-09T01:38:46.9648254Z

2026-04-09T01:38:46.9653580Z ◆ The `OperationContext.md` document explains the operation context system but doesn't specifically mention scope-based extension resources or the bug that was fixed. The commit fixes:
2026-04-09T01:38:46.9654944Z   
2026-04-09T01:38:46.9656597Z   1. **Generator (OperationContext.cs)**: Fixed stack push order for variable-key/variable-value pairs in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy`. Scope-based extension resources like ChaosTargets and KubernetesClusterExtensions were getting swapped Id.Name and Id.ResourceType.Type parameters.
2026-04-09T01:38:46.9658495Z   
2026-04-09T01:38:46.9660129Z   2. **Emitter (resource-metadata.ts)**: Fixed `getExpectedParentResourceType` to handle complex parent paths with more than 3 segments (e.g., `managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...`), enabling `ValidateResourceId` generation for these collections.
2026-04-09T01:38:46.9661836Z   
2026-04-09T01:38:46.9662045Z   
2026-04-09T01:38:46.9663715Z   The "Extension Resources" section in the docs is too shallow to capture the nuances of scope-based extension resources or how contextual parameters need to be ordered, and the emitter fix isn't documented anywhere yet. I should check what the emitter documentation currently covers.

This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. 2026-04-09T01:38:47.1611193Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-09T01:38:34.62792004Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"safeoutputs","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"body":"## Documentation Gap\n\n**Package:** `@azure-typespec/http-client-csharp-mgmt`\n**Service directory:** `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/`\n**Triggered by:** PR #57934 by @live1206\n\n## What Changed\n\nTwo bug fixes were merged for the Azure Management SDK generator/emitter:\n\n1. **Generator (`OperationContext.cs`):** Fixed a LIFO stack push-order bug in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy`. For scope-based extension resources (e.g., `ChaosTarget`, `KubernetesClusterExtension`), the stack was producing reversed positional order, causing `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` to be swapped in REST call arguments.\n\n2. **Emitter (`resource-metadata.ts`):** Fixed `getExpectedParentResourceType` to handle parent paths with more than 3 segments. For resources scoped under nested multi-provider paths (e.g., `managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...`), the function now falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the last `/providers/` segment, enabling `ValidateResourceId` generation for those collections.\n\n## Gaps Found\n\n- The `Extension Resources` section in `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (lines 162–168) is a two-sentence placeholder. It does not explain scope-based extension resources, how the `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy` LIFO stack resolves positional parameters for them, or the interaction between `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` for extension resource contexts.\n- There is no documentation describing how the emitter's `getExpectedParentResourceType` function computes the expected parent resource type for collection resources, the 3-segment rule, or the fallback to the last `/providers/` segment for multi-provider nested scopes.\n- There is no changelog entry for either fix in the `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/` package — there is no `CHANGELOG.md` at all in that directory.\n\n\u003cdetails\u003e\n\u003csummary\u003e\u003cstrong\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\u003c/strong\u003e\u003c/summary\u003e\n\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\n\n### Step 1: Modify files\n\n#### File 1: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (edit)\n\nExpand the **Extension Resources** section (currently lines ~162–168) to cover scope-based extension resources in detail. Replace the current terse section with:\n\n```markdown\n### Extension Resources\n\nExtension resources have a flexible scope — they can be attached to any Azure resource. Their contextual path starts with a single `resourceUri` variable:\n\n```\n/{resourceUri}/providers/Microsoft.Example/extensions/{extensionName}\n```\n\nThe `resourceUri` parameter is treated as a contextual parameter derived from the parent resource's `Id`.\n\n#### Scope-Based Extension Resources\n\nSome extension resources also capture the parent resource **type** as a contextual parameter in addition to its name. For example, a `ChaosTarget` contextual path looks like:\n\n```\n/{scope}/providers/Microsoft.Chaos/targets/{targetName}\n```\n\nWhen the generator builds `ContextualParameter` objects for such resources, each variable segment in the path is paired with the constant key that precedes it using a LIFO (stack-based) algorithm. The variable pairs are pushed onto the stack in **key-then-value** order so that the resulting positional list matches the path order from outermost to innermost scope.\n\nThe generated REST call correctly supplies arguments in this order:\n\n```csharp\n_restClient.Get(\n    Id.Parent.ResourceType.Type,  // e.g., \"Microsoft.Kubernetes/connectedClusters\"\n    Id.Parent.Name,               // e.g., \"myCluster\"\n    Id.Name,                      // e.g., \"myTarget\"\n    cancellationToken\n);\n```\n\nIf the stack push order were reversed (a previous bug), `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` would be swapped, producing incorrect REST calls.\n```\n\n#### File 2: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/docs/OperationContext.md` (edit)\n\nAdd a new subsection under **Advanced Scenarios** documenting the emitter's `ValidateResourceId` generation for complex parent paths. Add after the **Singleton Resources** subsection:\n\n```markdown\n### ValidateResourceId for Complex Multi-Provider Scopes\n\nWhen generating a resource collection, the emitter computes the **expected parent resource type** to emit a `ValidateResourceId` guard in the `GetAll` method. For simple paths (≤ 3 segments), the parent type is derived directly from the second-to-last provider segment.\n\nFor nested multi-provider paths with more than 3 segments — such as:\n\n```\nmanagementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/quotas\n```\n\nthe emitter falls back to computing the parent scope resource type from the **last `/providers/`** segment in the path. This ensures that `ValidateResourceId` is emitted correctly for these collection types even when the parent path has complex, multi-level scope nesting.\n```\n\n#### File 3: `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/CHANGELOG.md` (create)\n\nCreate a new CHANGELOG file to track generator/emitter changes:\n\n```markdown\n# Changelog\n\n## Unreleased\n\n### Bug Fixes\n\n- Fixed incorrect parameter ordering for scope-based extension resources (e.g., `ChaosTarget`, `KubernetesClusterExtension`). The LIFO stack in `BuildContextualParameterHierarchy` was reversed, swapping `Id.Name` and `Id.ResourceType.Type` in generated REST client arguments.\n- Fixed `ValidateResourceId` generation for resource collections whose parent paths contain more than 3 segments (nested multi-provider scopes such as `managementGroups/{mgId}/subscriptions/{subId}/providers/Microsoft.Quota/...`). The emitter now falls back to the last `/providers/` segment to compute the expected parent resource type.\n```\n\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\n\nNo README snippet placeholders are needed for these documentation-only changes (the docs are in `.md` files under `docs/`, not in a package README that uses the snippet generation tooling).\n\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\n\nThe `eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/README.md` is an npm package README (not a client library README), so the standard section checklist (Getting Started, Key Concepts, Examples, Troubleshooting, etc.) is not required. No changes needed to `README.md`.\n\n### Step 4: Validate\n\nThe docs changes are Markdown-only; no build or test commands are required. However, if any `.cs` source was touched, run:\n\n1. `dotnet build eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/`\n2. `dotnet test eng/packages/http-client-csharp-mgmt/generator/Azure.Generator.Management/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\n\n\u003c/details\u003e\n\n## Next Steps\n\n\u003e [!TIP]\n\u003e **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **@copilot** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\n","temporary_id":"aw_docsgap1","title":"[Azure Management Generator] Docs: Document scope-based extension resource parameter ordering and complex parent path ValidateResourceId generation"},"name":"create_issue"}}}

2026-04-09T01:38:47.2026955Z ##[group]Run # Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts
2026-04-09T01:38:47.2027220Z [36;1m# Fix permissions on firewall logs so they can be uploaded as artifacts[0m
2026-04-09T01:38:47.2027374Z [36;1m# AWF runs with sudo, creating files owned by root[0m
2026-04-09T01:38:47.2027578Z [36;1msudo chmod -R a+r /tmp/gh-aw/sandbox/firewall/logs 2>/dev/null || true[0m
